extractedFieldValues.current_value: The patient is considered homebound because he underwent extensive thoracic and cardiac surgery on 03/04/2026 (CABG x2, thymectomy, mass resection, pericardial and phrenic nerve resection, aortic arch adventitia resection, and diaphragm plication) and currently has healing surgical wounds with remaining stitches, left axillary swelling and minimal extremity edema that limit safe ambulation. Since discharge he reports postoperative pain managed with oxycodone PRN and requires daily insulin glargine administration (insulin regimen changed during hospitalization), creating a need for skilled medication management, glucose monitoring, and teaching. Exam documents decreased breath sounds after major thoracic procedures and the record indicates use of midodrine for low blood pressure and a history of paroxysmal atrial fibrillation, all of which increase cardiopulmonary risk with exertion. He is documented as 'At risk for falls' and uses a mobility device, indicating impaired safe community ambulation without assistance. Leaving home would require a considerable and taxing effort because ambulatory activity would provoke postoperative pain, exacerbate swelling, challenge recently altered respiratory mechanics, and risk hemodynamic instability or falls that would likely require emergency care. Allowable, limited absences are restricted to medically necessary appointments (e.g., scheduled cardiology/vascular follow-ups on 03/23/2026 and 04/13/2026 and endocrinology on 04/17/2026) or other brief, infrequent activities as tolerated.
source_spans: Primary Reason for Home Health / Medical Necessity The patient is considered homebound because he underwent extensive thoracic and cardiac surgery on 03/04/2026 (CABG x2, thymectomy, mass resection, pericardial and phrenic nerve resection, aortic arch adventitia resection, and diaphragm plication) and currently has healing surgical wounds with remaining stitches, left axillary swelling and minimal extremity edema that limit safe ambulation. Since discharge he reports postoperative pain managed with oxycodone PRN and requires daily insulin glargine administration (insulin regimen changed during hospitalization), creating a need for skilled medication management, glucose monitoring, and teaching. Exam documents decreased breath sounds after major thoracic procedures and the record indicates use of midodrine for low blood pressure and a history of paroxysmal atrial fibrillation, all of which increase cardiopulmonary risk with exertion. He is documented as 'At risk for falls' and uses a mobility device, indicating impaired safe community ambulation without assistance. Leaving home would require a considerable and taxing effort because ambulatory activity would provoke postoperative pain, exacerbate swelling, challenge recently altered respiratory mechanics, and risk hemodynamic instability or falls that would likely require emergency care. Allowable, limited absences are restricted to medically necessary appointments (e.g., scheduled cardiology/vascular follow-ups on 03/23/2026 and 04/13/2026 and endocrinology on 04/17/2026) or other brief, infrequent activities as tolerated. Taken together recent major thoracic/cardiac operations with ongoing wound healing and swelling, postoperative pain requiring opioid therapy, new insulin therapy requiring monitoring/teaching, documented fall risk, decreased breath sounds, and authorization for Home Health, these limitations meet Medicare homebound criteria and justify the medical necessity for skilled home health nursing and therapy services to manage wounds, monitor cardiopulmonary status, titrate medications, provide diabetes education, and reduce risk of readmission.
